description: Begging letter in Hebrew. In which the writer explains that he has arrived in Alexandria bringing three of his eight dependents with him. He is very ill and consequently reliant on the Jewish community’s charity, but has received very little support (only three zuzim) from the administrator of charity, the parnas. He wonders whether he has sinned against him or offended him in some way to deserve such poor treatment. He threatens that he may have to sell his ṭalleṯ, since he only has bread and onions in his house but he desires ‘meat and wine’. (Information from CUDL.)
description_he: מכתב בקשה בעברית. הכותב מסביר שהגיע לאלכסנדריה עם שלושה מתוך שמונה תלויים בו. הוא חולה מאוד ותלוי בצדקה הקהילתית אך קיבל מעט מאוד תמיכה. הוא מאיים שיצטרך למכור את טליתו
